findings: Found one new config-schema drift: the checked-in JSON Schema still models a retired config surface and rejects current keys used by the repo/runtime.

issues_found.title: Checked-in fabric-config JSON Schema still describes retired config surface
issues_found.description: `schemas/fabric-config.json` is stale: it sets `additionalProperties:false` but only allows old top-level keys, so the repo's current root `fabric.config.json` with `embed_enabled`/`embed_model` is schema-invalid, while the live TypeScript schema defines those fields. The same JSON Schema still allows retired v1 clientPaths (`windsurf`, `rooCode`, `geminiCLI`) and `externalFixturePath`, even though the TS schema says those client keys are rejected and dev fixture path now comes only from `EXTERNAL_FIXTURE_PATH`; docs also still list `externalFixturePath` as optional.
issues_found.fix_direction: Regenerate or remove `schemas/fabric-config.json` from the live `fabricConfigSchema`, including current fields such as embed/retrieval/store config, deleting retired v1 clients and `externalFixturePath`; also remove the stale `externalFixturePath` section from docs or mark it historical and point users to `EXTERNAL_FIXTURE_PATH`.
